name: UI Engineer
headline: Where design systems meet real components
description: Your feed covers component libraries, accessibility, and CSS architecture. Opinionated about design tokens and when to reach for a framework.

name: Web Perf Engineer
headline: Milliseconds are a product decision
description: Your feed surfaces Core Web Vitals, bundle analysis, and rendering strategies. You've strong feelings about when not to hydrate.

name: Mobile Engineer
headline: Native feel, cross-platform pragmatism
description: Your feed covers iOS and Android patterns, React Native trade-offs, and everything shipping to an app store. Push notifications are personal.

name: Full-Stack Builder
headline: From database schema to deploy button
description: Your feed blends API design, frontend frameworks, and deployment pipelines. You appreciate the satisfaction of owning the whole feature.

name: Cloud Platform Engineer
headline: Terraform applies and nobody flinches
description: Your feed covers Kubernetes, IaC, and cloud-provider internals. You care deeply about blast radius and the art of writing runbooks that actually get read.

name: SRE
headline: On-call is a design problem, not a ritual
description: Your feed surfaces observability tooling, incident retrospectives, and SLO frameworks. You define reliability as a feature, not a post-launch concern.

name: Security Engineer
headline: Ships fast and still sleeps at night
description: Your feed mixes appsec, supply-chain threats, and zero-days. You think threat modelling should start in sprint planning, not the week before launch.

name: Dev Tools Engineer
headline: Building the picks and shovels of software
description: Your feed covers compilers, build systems, CLIs, and developer experience improvements. You measure productivity in keystrokes saved per engineer per day.

name: ML Engineer
headline: Getting models out of notebooks and into production
description: Your feed covers model training, deployment, and evaluation frameworks. You've debugged enough shape mismatches to have opinions about tensor libraries.

name: AI App Builder
headline: LLMs as infrastructure, products as the goal
description: Your feed surfaces RAG architectures, prompt engineering, and emerging AI app patterns. You think the interesting layer is the one between the model and the user.

name: Data Engineer
headline: Reliable pipelines are the unsexy superpower
description: Your feed covers streaming, batch processing, and data-lake architecture. You have a war story involving a timezone bug that cost someone a quarter.

name: Analytics Engineer
headline: Turning queries into decisions, not just dashboards
description: Your feed mixes SQL craft, BI tooling, and data-modelling patterns. You believe a well-named metric definition is worth a thousand ad-hoc queries.

name: Game Developer
headline: Rendering 60fps and shipping before crunch
description: Your feed covers game engines, graphics programming, and the physics of fun. You benchmark shaders the way others benchmark APIs.

name: Embedded Engineer
headline: Code that runs where there is no OS
description: Your feed surfaces firmware patterns, RTOS trade-offs, and IoT protocols. You think in bytes because kilobytes are a luxury.

name: Engineering Leader
headline: Growing teams, not just code
description: Your feed covers org design, engineering culture, and delivery frameworks. You measure success by what your team ships when you are not in the room.

name: Technical Founder
headline: Building a company one hard decision at a time
description: Your feed mixes startup strategy, technical architecture, and growth tactics. You have shipped product, pitched investors, and debugged production on the same day.
